rawText:
### Recognize Documents Request

Source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/vision/recognizedocumentsrequest/supportedbarcodesymbologies

Details about the RecognizeDocumentsRequest and its properties.

```APIDOC
## struct RecognizeDocumentsRequest

### Description
Represents a request to recognize documents.

### Properties
- **supportedBarcodeSymbologies** (array of BarcodeSymbology) - Read-only - The collection of revisions the request supports.
- **barcodeDetectionOptions** (BarcodeDetectionOptions) - Configuration for detecting machine-readable codes in the document.
- **textRecognitionOptions** (TextRecognitionOptions) - Configuration for recognizing text in the document.
- **supportedRecognitionLanguages** (array of Locale.Language) - The identifiers of the languages that the request supports.
```

--------------------------------

### RecognizeDocumentsRequest.TextRecognitionOptions

Source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/vision/recognizedocumentsrequest/textrecognitionoptions-swift.struct

Configuration object for detected and recognized text within a document.

```APIDOC
## RecognizeDocumentsRequest.TextRecognitionOptions

### Description
A configuration object for detected and recognized text within the document.

### Properties
- **automaticallyDetectLanguage** (Bool) - A Boolean value that indicates whether to attempt detecting the language to use the appropriate model for recognition and language correction.
- **customWords** ([String]) - An array of strings to supplement the recognized languages at the word-recognition stage.
- **maximumCandidateCount** (Int) - The maximum number of text candidates to return.
- **minimumTextHeightFraction** (Float) - The minimum height, relative to the image height, of the text to recognize.
- **recognitionLanguages** ([Locale.Language]) - An array of languages to detect, in priority order.
- **useLanguageCorrection** (Bool) - A Boolean value that indicates whether the request applies language correction during the recognition process.

### Relationships
- **Conforms To**: Decodable, Encodable, Equatable, Hashable, Sendable, SendableMetatype
```

--------------------------------

### Create RecognizeDocumentsRequest

Source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/vision/recognizedocumentsrequest/init%28_%3A%29

Use this initializer to create a document content recognition request. Optionally specify a revision for the recognition algorithm.

```swift
init(_ revision: RecognizeDocumentsRequest.Revision? = nil)
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize RecognizeDocumentsRequest

Source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/vision/recognizedocumentsrequest/init%28_%3A%29

Creates a new instance of a document content recognition request with an optional revision parameter.

```APIDOC
## INITIALIZER RecognizeDocumentsRequest.init(_:)

### Description
Creates a document content recognition request.

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **revision** (RecognizeDocumentsRequest.Revision?) - Optional - The specific algorithm or implementation revision that performs the request.

### Request Example
```swift
let request = RecognizeDocumentsRequest(revision: .current)
```
```

--------------------------------

### RequestDescriptor.recognizeDocumentsRequest(_:)

Source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/vision/requestdescriptor/recognizedocumentsrequest%28_%3A%29

This method is used to recognize documents within an image. It requires JavaScript to be enabled.

```APIDOC
## RequestDescriptor.recognizeDocumentsRequest(_:)

### Description
Recognizes documents within an image. This method is available on iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, and visionOS versions 26.0 and later.

### Method
Not applicable (Swift case)

### Endpoint
Not applicable (Swift case)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```swift
case recognizeDocumentsRequest(RecognizeDocumentsRequest.Revision)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Not applicable (Swift case)

#### Response Example
Not applicable (Swift case)
```
